title: Change Success Rate Trending
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" earliest=-365d@d state="Closed"
| eval ok=if(match(lower(close_code),"(?i)successful|success|complete") OR match(lower(u_outcome),"(?i)success"),1,0)
| timechart span=1w sum(ok) as successes count as total
| eval success_pct=round(100*successes/nullif(total,0),1)
| trendline sma8(success_pct) as success_pct_trend
| eventstats median(success_pct) as med_qtr
| eval vs_median=round(success_pct - med_qtr,1)
description: A falling change success rate warns that testing, communication, or risk scoring is degrading before major outages; trending by week ties process fixes to measurable improvement.
value: A falling change success rate warns that testing, communication, or risk scoring is degrading before major outages; trending by week ties process fixes to measurable improvement.
implementation: Normalize `close_code` and `u_outcome` from ServiceNow; treat emergency and standard changes consistently in scope. Exclude duplicate closure events by deduping on `number` and `sys_updated_on`. Align week boundaries to your CAB calendar. Alert when `success_pct` drops below target for two consecutive weeks or falls more than 10 points under the trailing quarterly median.
visualization: Line chart (weekly success % with trendline), Single value (rolling 13-week average), Table (worst assignment groups).
knownFalsePositives: Change success that dips during big trains, new platforms, or a new way to record good versus bad in the form.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:change_request"`; optional `sourcetype=jira:*` change records.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Normalize `close_code` and `u_outcome` from ServiceNow; treat emergency and standard changes consistently in scope. Exclude duplicate closure events by deduping on `number` and `sys_updated_on`. Align week boundaries to your CAB calendar. Alert when `success_pct` drops below target for two consecutive weeks or falls more than 10 points under the trailing quarterly median.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" earliest=-365d@d state="Closed"
| eval ok=if(match(lower(close_code),"(?i)successful|success|complete") OR match(lower(u_outcome),"(?i)success"),1,0)
| timechart span=1w sum(ok) as successes count as total
| eval success_pct=round(100*successes/nullif(total,0),1)
| trendline sma8(success_pct) as success_pct_trend
| eventstats median(success_pct) as med_qtr
| eval vs_median=round(success_pct - med_qtr,1)
```

Understanding this SPL

**Change Success Rate Trending** — A falling change success rate warns that testing, communication, or risk scoring is degrading before major outages; trending by week ties process fixes to measurable improvement.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:change_request"`; optional `sourcetype=jira:*` change records. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:change_request. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:change_request", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ok** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1w** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **success_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Pipeline stage (see **Change Success Rate Trending**): trendline sma8(success_pct) as success_pct_trend
• `eventstats` aggregates the pipeline (counts, distinct values, sums, percentiles, etc.) into fewer rows.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **vs_median** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the Jira issue page, board, or service project view for the same dates. Confirm issue keys, status, and assignment fields; reconcile timestamp fields you use in the report.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (weekly success % with trendline), Single value (rolling 13-week average), Table (worst assignment groups).
grandmaExplanation: We trend change outcomes over time, so we can see a culture or platform get safer—or slip—before a bad release week.
